text:
*[Bug 18996] New: type of toeplitz(x=, r=) does not reflect type of 'r' when length(r) < 2*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18996>

            Bug ID: 18996
           Summary: type of toeplitz(x=, r=) does not reflect type of 'r'
                    when length(r) < 2
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Language
          Assignee: R-core@R-project.org
          Reporter: jaganmn@mcmaster.ca

A mishandled edge case that I notice while working on **Matrix**:

The type of `toeplitz(x=, r=)` should be the more general of the types of `x`
and `r`, even if no element of `r` is used (because `length(r)` is 0 or 1).

```
Index: src/library/stats/R/diffinv.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/stats/R/diffinv.R       (revision 89337)
+++ src/library/stats/R/diffinv.R       (working copy)
@@ -86,7 +86,7 @@
             warning("x[1] != r[1]; using x[1] for diagonal")
         ## toeplitz2(c(if(nc >= 2L) r[nc:2L], x), n, nc) :
         d <- c(n, nc)
-        array(c(if(nc >= 2L) r[nc:2L], x)[nc - .col(d) + .row(d)], d)
+        array(c(r[if(nc >= 2L) nc:2L else 0L], x)[nc - .col(d) + .row(d)], d)
     }
 }

```

I would test along these lines:

```
F <- function(tx, lx, tr, lr) {
    x <- vector(tx, lx)
    r <- vector(tr, lr)
    identical(toeplitz(x, r), matrix(c(FALSE, x[0L], r[0L]), lx, lr))
}
t3 <- c("integer", "double", "complex")
l3 <- 0L:2L
L <- expand.grid(tx=t3, lx=l3, tr=t3, lr=l3, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
stopifnot(all(unlist(.mapply(F, L, NULL))))
```
